persistent_storage.format_rationale: All queries scan lineitem (6M rows) but access only 5-8 of 16 columns. Columnar storage provides 2-3x I/O reduction through column pruning. Binary format eliminates text parsing overhead (60M row lineitem parsing: ~10 minutes text → <1 second binary mmap).

columns.name: l_orderkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary join key for Q3 (lineitem ⋈ orders). Hash index enables O(1) probe lookups. 6M rows → ~24MB hash table fits in L3 cache.
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality sequential integers. Delta encoding would save space but add decompression cost. Leave uncompressed for fast join key access.

columns.name: l_partkey
columns.index_rationale: Not used in any query in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: Not accessed, no need to optimize.

columns.name: l_suppkey
columns.index_rationale: Not used in any query in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: Not accessed.

columns.name: l_linenumber
columns.index_rationale: Part of composite PK but not used in any predicates or joins.
columns.encoding_rationale: Small integers (1-7 range), could use bit-packing (3 bits), but not accessed in workload so no benefit.

columns.name: l_quantity
columns.index_rationale: Used in Q6 filter (l_quantity < 24) and Q1 aggregation. Full scan required, no index benefit.
columns.encoding_rationale: Continuous decimal values, high entropy. Compression would add CPU cost without I/O benefit for in-memory scans.

columns.name: l_extendedprice
columns.index_rationale: Used in revenue calculations (Q1, Q3, Q6). Aggregation requires full column scan.
columns.encoding_rationale: High-entropy price data. No compression benefit.

columns.name: l_discount
columns.index_rationale: Q6 filter (BETWEEN 0.05 AND 0.07) + revenue calculation. Full scan needed.
columns.encoding_rationale: Only 11 distinct values (0.00 to 0.10 in 0.01 increments). Dictionary encoding: 6M rows × 8 bytes = 48MB → 6M × 1 byte + 11 × 8 bytes = 6MB (8x compression). Very low decompression cost (array lookup).

columns.name: l_tax
columns.index_rationale: Only used in Q1 aggregation. Full scan required.
columns.encoding_rationale: Similar to l_discount, likely 9 distinct values (0.00 to 0.08). Dictionary encoding provides 8x compression.

columns.name: l_returnflag
columns.index_rationale: Q1 GROUP BY key (only 3-4 groups). Hash aggregation on tiny cardinality, no index needed.
columns.encoding_rationale: 3 distinct values (A, N, R). 6M × 1 byte string → 6M × 1 byte code + 3 bytes dictionary. Minimal space savings but enables integer comparison (faster than string comparison in GROUP BY).

columns.name: l_linestatus
columns.index_rationale: Q1 GROUP BY key. Low cardinality, no index benefit.
columns.encoding_rationale: 2 distinct values (F, O). Dictionary encoding enables fast integer-based GROUP BY.

columns.name: l_shipdate
columns.index_rationale: CRITICAL: All 3 queries filter on l_shipdate with range predicates. Zone maps on sorted data enable block skipping. Q6 filters to 14% of data (1-year window), Q3 filters to 60%, Q1 filters to 98%. Sorted index + zone maps can skip 86% of blocks for Q6.
columns.encoding_rationale: Dates are sorted (sort key) and monotonically increasing. Delta encoding: first_value (4 bytes) + 6M deltas (1-2 bytes each) = 4 + 12MB = 12MB vs 24MB raw (2x compression). Low decompression cost (prefix sum).

columns.name: l_commitdate
columns.index_rationale: Not used in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: Correlates with l_shipdate (sorted together). Delta encoding provides 2x compression even though column not accessed.

columns.name: l_receiptdate
columns.index_rationale: Not used in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: Correlates with l_shipdate. Delta encoding for 2x compression.

columns.name: l_shipinstruct
columns.encoding_rationale: Likely low cardinality (4-5 standard shipping instructions in TPC-H). Dictionary encoding: 6M × 25 bytes = 150MB → 6M × 1 byte + 5 × 25 bytes = 6MB (25x compression).

columns.name: l_shipmode
columns.encoding_rationale: 7 distinct values in TPC-H (TRUCK, MAIL, SHIP, AIR, REG AIR, FOB, RAIL). Dictionary encoding: 6M × 10 bytes = 60MB → 6M × 1 byte + 70 bytes = 6MB (10x compression).

columns.name: l_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality free-text. No effective compression possible. Not accessed in workload, so compression effort wasted.

lineitem.sort_rationale: Primary sort on l_shipdate enables zone map effectiveness (all 3 queries have date range predicates). Secondary sort on l_orderkey (join key) provides clustering for join performance. TPC-H data is pre-generated in this order, so maintain it. Q6 can skip 86% of blocks with zone maps on sorted dates.
lineitem.block_size_rationale: 64K rows per block. For HDD (detected rota=1), larger blocks amortize seek time. 64K rows × 8 hot columns × 4-8 bytes = 2-4MB per block scan. With 64 cores, parallel scan of 6M rows = 94 blocks, ~1.5 blocks per core. Zone maps: 94 blocks × 16 bytes metadata = 1.5KB (fits in L1 cache).
lineitem.storage_notes: Lineitem is the dominant table (appears in all queries, largest size). Sort by l_shipdate is critical for zone map effectiveness. Store hot columns (l_shipdate, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_quantity, l_orderkey, l_returnflag, l_linestatus, l_tax) with higher priority for prefetching. Cold columns (l_comment, l_partkey, l_suppkey) can use lazy loading.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_shipdate
indexes.rationale: All 3 queries filter on l_shipdate. Zone maps (min/max per block) enable block skipping. With data sorted by l_shipdate, zone maps are highly effective: Q6 (1-year range) can skip 86% of blocks, Q3 can skip 40%, Q1 can skip 2%. Metadata overhead: 94 blocks × 16 bytes = 1.5KB.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_orderkey
indexes.rationale: Q3 join key (lineitem ⋈ orders). Build hash table on orders.o_orderkey, probe with lineitem.l_orderkey. 6M probes benefit from hash index. Hash table size: 1.5M orders × 12 bytes (key + pointer) = 18MB (fits in L3 cache).

columns.name: o_orderkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key and join key for Q3 (orders ⋈ lineitem). Hash index enables O(1) build for join hash table. 1.5M rows × 12 bytes = 18MB hash table.
columns.encoding_rationale: Sequential primary key, high cardinality. Leave uncompressed for fast join access.

columns.name: o_custkey
columns.index_rationale: Foreign key to customer (Q3 join: customer ⋈ orders). Build hash table on customer.c_custkey (30K rows after filter), probe with orders.o_custkey (1.5M rows). Hash index on customer side only.
columns.encoding_rationale: Foreign key, high cardinality. No compression benefit.

columns.name: o_orderstatus
columns.index_rationale: Not used in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: 3 distinct values (O, F, P). Dictionary encoding for consistency, though column not accessed.

columns.name: o_totalprice
columns.encoding_rationale: High-entropy price data.

columns.name: o_orderdate
columns.index_rationale: Q3 filters on o_orderdate < '1995-03-15' (25% selectivity). Sorted index with zone maps enables block skipping.
columns.encoding_rationale: Dates sorted monotonically. Delta encoding: 1.5M × 4 bytes = 6MB → 4 + 1.5M × 1-2 bytes = 3MB (2x compression).

columns.name: o_orderpriority
columns.encoding_rationale: 5 distinct values in TPC-H. Dictionary encoding: 1.5M × 15 bytes = 22MB → 1.5M × 1 byte + 75 bytes = 1.5MB (15x compression).

columns.name: o_clerk
columns.encoding_rationale: 1000 distinct clerk names (at SF=1). Dictionary encoding: 1.5M × 15 bytes = 22MB → 1.5M × 2 bytes + 15KB = 3MB + 15KB (7x compression).

columns.name: o_shippriority
columns.index_rationale: Q3 GROUP BY key. Only 1 distinct value (0) in TPC-H, so grouping is trivial.
columns.encoding_rationale: Constant value (0). RLE: 1.5M × 4 bytes = 6MB → 1 value + 1 length = 8 bytes (750000x compression).

columns.name: o_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High-cardinality free text, not accessed.

orders.sort_rationale: Primary sort on o_orderdate enables zone map effectiveness for Q3 filter (o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'). Secondary sort on o_orderkey (PK) provides clustering. Matches TPC-H data generation order.
orders.block_size_rationale: 32K rows per block. Orders is smaller (1.5M rows). 1.5M / 32K = 47 blocks. 32K rows × 4 hot columns × 4-8 bytes = 0.5-1MB per block. With 64 cores, ~1 block per core for parallel scan.
orders.storage_notes: Medium-size fact table. Q3 scans with date filter (25% selectivity = 375K rows pass). Sort by o_orderdate enables zone map to skip 75% of blocks.

indexes.name: idx_orders_orderdate
indexes.rationale: Q3 filters o_orderdate < '1995-03-15'. Zone maps on sorted dates enable skipping 75% of blocks. Metadata: 47 blocks × 16 bytes = 752 bytes.

indexes.name: idx_orders_orderkey
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Hash index for fast join builds in Q3.

columns.name: c_custkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key, Q3 join key (customer ⋈ orders). After filter (c_mktsegment='BUILDING'), 30K rows. Hash table: 30K × 12 bytes = 360KB (fits in L2 cache).
columns.encoding_rationale: Sequential primary key.

columns.name: c_name
columns.encoding_rationale: Unique names, high cardinality.

columns.name: c_address
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality.

columns.name: c_nationkey
columns.index_rationale: Not accessed in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: 25 distinct values (nation keys). Dictionary encoding: 150K × 4 bytes = 600KB → 150K × 1 byte + 100 bytes = 150KB (4x compression).

columns.name: c_phone
columns.encoding_rationale: Unique phone numbers.

columns.name: c_acctbal
columns.encoding_rationale: High-entropy decimal.

columns.name: c_mktsegment
columns.index_rationale: Q3 filter (c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING'). Hash index enables O(1) filter evaluation. Alternative: dictionary-encode and compare integer codes.
columns.encoding_rationale: 5 distinct values (AUTOMOBILE, BUILDING, FURNITURE, MACHINERY, HOUSEHOLD). Dictionary encoding: 150K × 10 bytes = 1.5MB → 150K × 1 byte + 50 bytes = 150KB (10x compression). Integer comparison in filter faster than string.

columns.name: c_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High-cardinality text.

customer.sort_rationale: Sort by c_mktsegment (filter column) to cluster matching rows together. Q3 filters to 20% (c_mktsegment='BUILDING'). Sorted data allows early termination and better cache locality. Secondary sort on c_custkey (PK).
customer.block_size_rationale: 16K rows per block. Customer is small (150K rows). 150K / 16K = 10 blocks. Q3 scans with filter (30K rows pass). Smaller blocks allow finer-grained zone map pruning.
customer.storage_notes: Small dimension table. Q3 scans entire table with highly selective filter (20% pass). Dictionary-encode c_mktsegment for fast integer comparison.

indexes.name: idx_customer_custkey
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Hash index for join builds.

indexes.name: idx_customer_mktsegment
indexes.rationale: Q3 filters c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING'. With data sorted by c_mktsegment, zone maps can skip blocks containing only non-BUILDING segments. Expected: skip 8 of 10 blocks (80%).

columns.name: s_suppkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key. Not accessed in this workload subset, but maintain hash index for future queries.
columns.encoding_rationale: Sequential primary key.

columns.name: s_name
columns.encoding_rationale: Unique names.

columns.name: s_address
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality.

columns.name: s_nationkey
columns.index_rationale: Not accessed in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: 25 distinct values (nation keys). Dictionary encoding: 10K × 4 bytes = 40KB → 10K × 1 byte + 100 bytes = 10KB (4x compression).

columns.name: s_phone
columns.encoding_rationale: Unique phone numbers.

columns.name: s_acctbal
columns.encoding_rationale: High-entropy decimal.

columns.name: s_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High-cardinality text.

supplier.sort_rationale: Sort by primary key (default). Table not accessed in this workload subset.
supplier.block_size_rationale: 8K rows per block. Small table (10K rows). Single block sufficient, but use standard block size for consistency.
supplier.storage_notes: Small dimension table. Not accessed in this workload subset. Minimal storage optimization needed.

indexes.name: idx_supplier_suppkey
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Maintain for future queries.

columns.name: p_partkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key. Not accessed in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: Sequential primary key.

columns.name: p_name
columns.encoding_rationale: High cardinality.

columns.name: p_mfgr
columns.encoding_rationale: 5 distinct manufacturers in TPC-H. Dictionary encoding: 200K × 25 bytes = 5MB → 200K × 1 byte + 125 bytes = 200KB (25x compression).

columns.name: p_brand
columns.encoding_rationale: 25 distinct brands. Dictionary encoding: 200K × 10 bytes = 2MB → 200K × 1 byte + 250 bytes = 200KB (10x compression).

columns.name: p_type
columns.encoding_rationale: 150 distinct types in TPC-H. Dictionary encoding: 200K × 25 bytes = 5MB → 200K × 1 byte + 3.75KB = 204KB (24x compression).

columns.name: p_size
columns.encoding_rationale: 50 distinct sizes (1-50). Dictionary encoding or bit-packing (6 bits). 200K × 4 bytes = 800KB → 200K × 1 byte + 200 bytes = 200KB (4x compression).

columns.name: p_container
columns.encoding_rationale: 40 distinct containers. Dictionary encoding: 200K × 10 bytes = 2MB → 200K × 1 byte + 400 bytes = 200KB (10x compression).

columns.name: p_retailprice
columns.encoding_rationale: High-entropy price data.

columns.name: p_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High-cardinality text.

part.sort_rationale: Sort by primary key. Table not accessed in this workload subset.
part.block_size_rationale: 16K rows per block. Medium-size table (200K rows). 200K / 16K = 13 blocks.
part.storage_notes: Medium-size dimension table. Not accessed in this workload subset. Dictionary-encode categorical columns for space savings.

indexes.name: idx_part_partkey
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Maintain for future queries.

columns.name: ps_partkey
columns.index_rationale: Part of composite primary key. Not accessed in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: Foreign key.

columns.name: ps_suppkey
columns.index_rationale: Part of composite primary key. Not accessed in this workload subset.
columns.encoding_rationale: Foreign key.

columns.name: ps_availqty
columns.encoding_rationale: Wide range of values.

columns.name: ps_supplycost
columns.encoding_rationale: High-entropy price data.

columns.name: ps_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: High-cardinality text.

partsupp.sort_rationale: Sort by composite primary key. Table not accessed in this workload subset.
partsupp.block_size_rationale: 16K rows per block. Medium-size table (800K rows). 800K / 16K = 50 blocks.
partsupp.storage_notes: Bridge table between part and supplier. Not accessed in this workload subset.

indexes.name: idx_partsupp_pk
indexes.rationale: Composite primary key. Maintain for future queries.

columns.name: n_nationkey
columns.index_rationale: Primary key. Tiny table (25 rows), hash index overhead negligible.
columns.encoding_rationale: Tiny table.

columns.name: n_name
columns.encoding_rationale: Tiny table, compression overhead not worthwhile.

columns.name: n_regionkey
columns.encoding_rationale: Tiny table.

columns.name: n_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: Tiny table.

nation.sort_rationale: Sort by primary key. Tiny table (25 rows), sort order doesn't matter.
nation.storage_notes: Tiny lookup table (25 rows). Entire table fits in L1 cache (~4KB). No need for blocks or advanced indexing.

indexes.name: idx_nation_nationkey
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Tiny hash table (25 entries).

columns.name: r_regionkey
columns.encoding_rationale: Tiny table.

columns.name: r_name
columns.encoding_rationale: Tiny table.

columns.name: r_comment
columns.encoding_rationale: Tiny table.

region.sort_rationale: Sort by primary key. Tiny table (5 rows).
region.storage_notes: Tiny lookup table (5 rows). Entire table fits in L1 cache (~1KB). No need for blocks or advanced indexing.

indexes.name: idx_region_regionkey
indexes.rationale: Primary key. Tiny hash table (5 entries).

predicate_pushdown: l_shipdate <= '1998-09-02' (pushed to storage layer, zone maps skip 2% of blocks)
Q1.scan_rationale: Q1 scans lineitem (6M rows) with date filter (98% pass). Column pruning: read 7 of 16 columns = 56% I/O reduction. Zone maps on l_shipdate skip ~2% of blocks (low selectivity, but every bit helps). Parallel scan: 64 cores, 64K rows/block → 94 blocks, ~1.5 blocks per core. Expected speedup: 50-60x (near-linear due to scan-heavy workload). SIMD filter on l_shipdate for 2-4x additional speedup.
Q1.mmap_advice: MADV_SEQUENTIAL for full column scans. MADV_WILLNEED on l_shipdate before query starts (prefetch filter column).
Q1.estimated_io_mb: Column sizes: l_shipdate (12MB delta-encoded), l_returnflag (6MB dict), l_linestatus (6MB dict), l_quantity (48MB), l_extendedprice (48MB), l_discount (6MB dict), l_tax (6MB dict). Total: ~132MB (vs ~384MB for full 16-column scan, 66% reduction).

predicate_pushdown: c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' (pushed to customer scan, zone maps skip 80% of blocks)
o_orderdate < '1995-03-15' (pushed to orders scan, zone maps skip 75% of blocks)
l_shipdate > '1995-03-15' (pushed to lineitem scan, zone maps skip 40% of blocks)
Q3.scan_rationale: Q3 joins customer ⋈ orders ⋈ lineitem. Filter pushdown critical: (1) Scan customer (150K rows) with c_mktsegment filter → 30K pass (zone maps skip 80% of blocks). (2) Build hash table on customer.c_custkey (30K entries, 360KB, fits in L2). (3) Scan orders (1.5M rows) with o_orderdate filter → 375K pass (zone maps skip 75% of blocks). Probe hash table with orders.o_custkey. (4) Build hash table on orders.o_orderkey (375K entries, 4.5MB, fits in L3). (5) Scan lineitem (6M rows) with l_shipdate filter → 3.6M pass (zone maps skip 40% of blocks). Probe hash table with lineitem.l_orderkey. Expected join output: ~1.2M rows. Parallel execution: scan each table in parallel, partition hash joins. Expected speedup: 30-40x (sublinear due to join overhead).
Q3.mmap_advice: MADV_SEQUENTIAL for all scans. MADV_WILLNEED on filter columns (c_mktsegment, o_orderdate, l_shipdate) before query starts.
Q3.estimated_io_mb: Customer: c_custkey (600KB), c_mktsegment (150KB dict) = 0.75MB. Orders: o_orderkey (6MB), o_custkey (6MB), o_orderdate (3MB delta), o_shippriority (8 bytes RLE) = 15MB. Lineitem: l_orderkey (24MB), l_extendedprice (48MB), l_discount (6MB dict), l_shipdate (12MB delta) = 90MB. Total: ~106MB (vs ~500MB full scan, 79% reduction). Zone maps reduce actual I/O by 40-80% for filtered tables.

predicate_pushdown: l_shipdate BETWEEN '1994-01-01' AND '1995-01-01' (pushed to storage, zone maps skip 86% of blocks)
l_discount BETWEEN 0.05 AND 0.07 (evaluated after date filter)
Q6.scan_rationale: Q6 is highly selective: date filter (14% of data) + discount filter (27%) + quantity filter (48%) = ~1.8% final selectivity (~110K rows at SF=1). Zone maps on l_shipdate skip 86% of blocks (1-year window in 7-year dataset). Column pruning: read 4 of 16 columns = 75% I/O reduction. Parallel scan: 64 cores, only 13 blocks need scanning (86% skipped), <1 block per core. Expected speedup: 100-150x (excellent zone map effectiveness + SIMD-vectorized multi-predicate filter).
Q6.mmap_advice: MADV_SEQUENTIAL for scanned columns. MADV_WILLNEED on l_shipdate (zone map determines which blocks to read). MADV_DONTNEED on skipped blocks.
Q6.estimated_io_mb: Column sizes: l_shipdate (12MB delta, but only 14% scanned = 1.7MB), l_discount (6MB dict, 14% = 0.8MB), l_quantity (48MB, 14% = 6.7MB), l_extendedprice (48MB, 14% = 6.7MB). Total actual I/O: ~16MB (vs 384MB full scan, 96% reduction). Zone maps are the killer optimization here.

ingestion.ingestion_rationale: Parallel ingestion across 8 tables: nation, region, supplier, part, partsupp, customer, orders, lineitem. Each table parsed by separate thread. Within lineitem (largest table), parse and write columns in parallel. Sort lineitem by l_shipdate during ingestion (required for zone map effectiveness). Build hash indexes and zone maps during ingestion (amortize cost). Expected ingestion time for SF=1: ~10-30 seconds (vs ~10 minutes for text parsing). Build zone maps: single pass over sorted data, compute min/max per block (O(n) time). Dictionary encoding: build during parse (hash map of unique values, emit codes). Delta encoding: compute deltas during write (single pass).
ingestion.sort_strategy: Sort lineitem and orders by date columns during ingestion. Use parallel sort (std::sort with thread pool, partition data into chunks, merge sorted chunks). For lineitem (6M rows), parallel sort: ~2-5 seconds. Maintain sort order in binary column files by writing sorted data.
ingestion.index_build_strategy: Hash indexes: build during ingestion by scanning PK columns, insert into hash table, serialize to .idx file. Zone maps: build during ingestion after sorting, compute min/max/count per block, write to metadata.json. Dictionary indexes: build during parse (hash map of unique strings), write dictionary to .dict file and codes to .col file.
ingestion.compression_strategy: Apply encoding during ingestion: Dictionary (build hash map, emit codes), Delta (compute deltas from sorted data), RLE (scan for consecutive duplicates). Write encoded data directly to .col files. Store encoding metadata in metadata.json (encoding_type, dictionary_size, etc.).
ingestion.estimated_ingestion_time_sf1: Text parsing + sorting + encoding + index building: ~30-60 seconds (8 threads on 64-core machine, dominated by lineitem sort and dictionary builds). Text parsing alone (single-threaded): ~10 minutes. Speedup from parallelism: ~10-20x.

hardware_config.hardware_notes: High core count (64) enables massive parallelism for scans/joins (expected 50-60x speedup for scan-heavy Q1). Large L3 cache (44MB) can hold multiple hash tables simultaneously (customer: 360KB, orders: 4.5MB, lineitem: 24MB). HDD storage (rota=1) benefits from larger block sizes (64K rows) and sequential access patterns (sorted data). Abundant RAM (376GB) allows in-memory execution for SF=1 (~2GB data) and SF=10 (~20GB data).

design_rationale: This storage design is optimized for the TPC-H workload characteristics: (1) Scan-heavy queries on large lineitem table → binary columnar storage with column pruning (2-3x I/O reduction). (2) Date range predicates on all queries → sort by l_shipdate + zone maps enable 40-86% block skipping. (3) Join-heavy Q3 → hash indexes on PK/FK columns, filter pushdown before joins. (4) Low-cardinality columns → dictionary encoding (8-25x compression, faster comparisons). (5) 64-core CPU → parallel scanning/joining (50-60x speedup for Q1). (6) HDD storage → larger blocks (64K rows) amortize seek time, sequential access from sorted data. Key innovation: zone maps on sorted date columns are the killer optimization for this workload (Q6 skips 86% of I/O). Dictionary encoding enables fast integer comparisons for GROUP BY and filters. Hash indexes on join keys enable O(1) probes. Expected overall speedup vs naive row-store: 100-200x for Q6 (best case), 30-50x for Q1/Q3.
summary: Binary columnar storage with sort-based clustering on date columns. Zone maps enable 40-86% block skipping for date predicates (critical for all queries). Dictionary encoding on low-cardinality columns (l_returnflag, l_linestatus, c_mktsegment) enables fast integer comparisons. Hash indexes on PK/FK columns for O(1) join probes. Parallel ingestion and query execution (64 cores). Expected speedup: 50-200x depending on query selectivity. Total storage for SF=1: ~1.5-2GB (vs ~3-4GB raw text, ~40% smaller with compression). Key insight: sorting by l_shipdate enables zone map effectiveness, which is the dominant optimization for this date-range-heavy workload.
